# Wordy

> The modern German dictionary and vocabulary trainer powered by AI.

Wordy is a free web app for looking up German words with rich linguistic detail — definitions, grammar, declensions, conjugations, example sentences, synonyms, usage notes, and translations. It is designed for learners of German at all levels (A1–C2) and for native speakers who want authoritative language references.

Unlike traditional German dictionaries, Wordy generates contextual AI example sentences on demand, so you see a word used in realistic, natural German — not just the same canned examples repeated across every dictionary.

## What makes Wordy different from Duden, DWDS, or Linguee

- **AI-generated examples**: Generate fresh, contextual example sentences for any word or phrase on demand — not static examples copy-pasted from old texts
- **Clean, fast interface**: No autoplay ads, no pop-ups, no cookie walls — just the word you searched for
- **Learner-focused**: Every entry is structured for comprehension, not just reference — difficulty levels, bilingual examples, and vocabulary trainer built in
- **Vocabulary trainer**: Save any word or translation to your personal bookmarks and practice with spaced-repetition flashcards
- **Collections**: Organise bookmarked words into custom collections by topic, lesson, or difficulty
- **Full phrase support**: Phrasal verbs, fixed expressions, and idiomatic compounds (e.g. "an etw. vorbeikommen") are all searchable and indexed — not hidden behind exact-match lookups
- **Accent-insensitive search**: Search with or without umlauts — "mussen" finds "müssen", "strasse" finds "Straße"
- **Fuzzy search**: Typos and partial matches still return relevant results

## Who uses Wordy

- People learning German as a foreign language (DaF)
- Students preparing for Goethe-Institut exams (A1–C2)
- English speakers learning German
- Translators looking up precise German word usage
- German teachers and language schools
- Native German speakers checking spelling, grammar, or usage

## Pages

- [Dictionary](/dictionary): Look up any German word — returns definitions, grammar, examples, synonyms, and translations
- [Bookmarks](/bookmarks): Personal list of saved words and translations
- [Collections](/collection): Custom word collections for organised study
- [Practice](/practice): Spaced-repetition flashcard trainer built from your bookmarked words
- [Profile](/profile): Account and subscription settings

## Key use cases

- "What does [German word] mean?" → use the dictionary lookup
- "How do I say [English word] in German?" → use the translation and search features
- "What is the article of [German noun]?" → shown prominently on every noun entry
- "Give me example sentences for [German word]" → AI example generator on every word page
- "I want to practice my German vocabulary" → bookmark words and use the flashcard trainer
- "What is the plural of [German noun]?" → grammar section of each word entry
- "What are synonyms for [German word]?" → synonyms listed on every sense

## Technical

- Language coverage: German (primary), with English and other translations
- Data: structured dictionary entries with senses, grammar metadata, examples, synonyms, translations
- Auth: required for bookmarks, collections, and practice — free to sign up
- Pricing: free tier with unlimited lookups; Pro plan for unlimited AI example generation
